articles.title: Maine governor targets oyster farmer in competitive Democratic Senate primary
articles.description:
Janet Mills, the two-term governor, is running ads in the state slamming insurgent Graham Platner’s past Reddit comments on sexual assault.

The post Maine governor targets oyster farmer in competitive Democratic Senate primary appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Maine Governor Janet Mills targets Graham Platner in Senate primary ads.
Platner's past Reddit comments on sexual assault are under scrutiny.
Boston residents may see implications in regional political dynamics.

articles.title: Trump team applying pressure to media: Tell the war’s story the way we see it
articles.description:
The Republican president has fumed on social media about stories he doesn't like and berated a reporter on Air Force One.

The post Trump team applying pressure to media: Tell the war’s story the way we see it appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Trump's team pressures media to align coverage with their narrative on war.
President criticized reporters on Air Force One, escalating tensions with the press.
Boston residents may see biased reporting affecting local perceptions of national issues.

articles.title: After serving prison time, Andrea James became a leading criminal justice advocate. Now she’s running for governor.
articles.description:
"It's time we start using the tremendous wealth in this state to invest in people's needs."

The post After serving prison time, Andrea James became a leading criminal justice advocate. Now she’s running for governor. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Andrea James, a former inmate, advocates for criminal justice reform in Massachusetts.
James emphasizes investing state wealth in community needs for better public welfare.
Her gubernatorial run could influence policies affecting Boston residents' justice and equity.

articles.title: ‘The sun will rise, the sun will set, and I’ll have lunch’: The timeless wisdom of Lou Gorman
articles.description:
Gorman's levity when Roger Clemens walked out of camp proved the right approach.

The post ‘The sun will rise, the sun will set, and I’ll have lunch’: The timeless wisdom of Lou Gorman appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Lou Gorman's approach helped manage tensions during Roger Clemens' departure from the Red Sox.
Gorman served as Red Sox GM from 1984 to 1993, influencing team culture.
His wisdom continues to resonate with Boston fans, emphasizing resilience in sports.
